root_text.sn56.51:1.1: Atha kho bhagavā parittaṁ nakhasikhāyaṁ paṁsuṁ āropetvā bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.sn56.51:1.3: katamaṁ nu kho bahutaraṁ—yo vāyaṁ mayā paritto nakhasikhāyaṁ paṁsu āropito, ayaṁ vā mahāpathavī”ti?
root_text.sn56.51:1.4: “Etadeva, bhante, bahutaraṁ yadidaṁ—mahāpathavī; appamattakāyaṁ bhagavatā paritto nakhasikhāyaṁ paṁsu āropito.
root_text.sn56.51:1.5: Saṅkhampi na upeti, upanidhampi na upeti, kalabhāgampi na upeti mahāpathaviṁ upanidhāya bhagavatā paritto nakhasikhāyaṁ paṁsu āropito”ti.
root_text.sn56.51:1.6: “Evameva kho, bhikkhave, ariyasāvakassa diṭṭhisampannassa puggalassa abhisametāvino etadeva bahutaraṁ dukkhaṁ yadidaṁ parikkhīṇaṁ pariyādinnaṁ; appamattakaṁ avasiṭṭhaṁ.
root_text.sn56.51:1.7: Saṅkhampi na upeti, upanidhampi na upeti, kalabhāgampi na upeti purimaṁ dukkhakkhandhaṁ parikkhīṇaṁ pariyādinnaṁ upanidhāya yadidaṁ sattakkhattuparamatā;
root_text.sn56.51:1.8: yo ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.sn56.51:2.1: Tasmātiha, bhikkhave, ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yogo karaṇīyo …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yogo karaṇīyo”ti.

translation_text.sn56.51:1.1: Then the Buddha, picking up a little bit of dirt on his fingernail, addressed the mendicants:
translation_text.sn56.51:1.3: Which is more: the little bit of dirt on my fingernail, or this great earth?”
translation_text.sn56.51:1.4: “Sir, the great earth is certainly more. The little bit of dirt on your fingernail is tiny.
translation_text.sn56.51:1.5: Compared to the great earth, it doesn’t count, there’s no comparison, it’s not worth a fraction.”
translation_text.sn56.51:1.6: “In the same way, for an individual with comprehension, a noble disciple accomplished in view, the suffering that’s over and done with is more, what’s left is tiny.
translation_text.sn56.51:1.7: Compared to the mass of suffering in the past that’s over and done with, it doesn’t count, there’s no comparison, it’s not worth a fraction, since there are at most seven more lives.
translation_text.sn56.51:1.8: Such an individual truly understands about suffering, its origin, its cessation, and the path.
translation_text.sn56.51:2.1: That’s why you should practice meditation …”
